---
title: "Klima Protocol: Fee vs. Principal"
subtitle: "Three ways to price a carbon-retirement desk"
format:
  revealjs:
    theme: simple
    slide-number: true
    incremental: false
    embed-resources: true
---

## The fee-based model

**Charge a markup on the token cost and pass the token through.**

- No inventory, no balance sheet, no capital required.
- Revenue is always positive — there is no loss tail.
- Upside scales with token-price activity: healthy in rallies,
  thin in quiet markets; never more than the markup.

::: {.fragment .fade-in}
*The safe baseline — zero downside, zero capital, zero leverage
over pricing.*
:::

## The custom desk model

**Quote a fixed USD price per tonne up-front and pre-buy part of
the tokens in advance.**

- The client price is locked; the desk absorbs the spot risk.
- Pre-bought inventory hedges the early part of the contract;
  the uncovered tail is still exposed if prices rise.
- At full coverage the terminal P&L is deterministic —
  predictable, but capital is locked up up-front.

::: {.fragment .fade-in}
*A dial between "fully hedged and deterministic" and "unhedged
and exposed" — inventory size sets the risk appetite.*
:::

## The switching model

**Quote a fixed price normally, but flip to fee-mode pricing
whenever the token runs hot.**

- Caps the loss on rally paths — the worst-case tail is
  truncated.
- Keeps principal-style margins on calm paths.
- One dial (the threshold): tighter cuts the tail harder but
  drags average revenue down; looser behaves more like plain
  principal.

::: {.fragment .fade-in}
*A dynamic collar — harvest principal margins in calm markets
and auto-downshift to fee when the market runs.*
:::
